 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Blocking pool execution semantics","description":"Goal: blocking pool execution semantics (capacity, fairness, cancellation, priority). Ensure no task leaks and correct shutdown. Include unit tests for queueing, cancellation, and shutdown behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:33:34.274766938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T06:45:05.843523705Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T06:45:05.843457061Z","close_reason":"Implemented blocking pool with capacity management, FIFO queueing, soft cancellation, graceful shutdown. Wired into RuntimeBuilder/Runtime/RuntimeHandle with spawn_blocking() API. All unit tests (10) and integration tests (2) pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["blocking","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-1j64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32n6","title":"[ALIEN][E2E][HOMO] End-to-end: topology-guided exploration run + coverage report","description":"## Goal

End-to-end validation that persistent-homology scoring (topological novelty) improves schedule exploration *as a user experiences it*:
- explorer runs deterministically
- prioritization is explainable (evidence ledger)
- we get better bug-finding / coverage per explored node than baseline ordering

This harness complements unit tests for complexes/persistence; it validates the whole pipeline.

## Inputs

- A fixed corpus of deterministic lab scenarios (same as / compatible with bd-1ny4)
- ExplorerConfig knobs:
  - baseline: FIFO / existing ordering
  - topo: PH-guided ordering

## Required outputs (deterministic artifacts)

- A report per scenario and aggregate summary (JSON preferred) including:
  - explored nodes until first violation (if any)
  - equivalence-class count discovered
  - novelty score distribution over time
  - top-k "most novel" nodes with ledger snippets
  - runtime overhead attribution (time spent scoring vs executing)

## Logging constraints

- No stdout/stderr from core runtime code.
- Use trace events (Cx::trace) for internal scoring decisions; the harness may print a summary.

## Acceptance criteria

- Deterministic: identical outputs across repeated runs (same seed/config).
- Demonstrates a measurable improvement on at least one scenario (fewer nodes to failure OR higher coverage at same budget).
- Includes failure-mode reporting when scoring is disabled/falls back (e.g., analysis too expensive).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:03.025357317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:05:00.846357566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:05:00.846292956Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic topology E2E coverage-report test in tests/topology_benchmark.rs","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","e2e","lab","testing","topology","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-115y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-1ny4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3490","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-3oqu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32n6","depends_on_id":"bd-453l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:16Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-32ot","title":"E2E: Multi-Protocol Integration Tests","description":"E2E tests exercising multiple protocol stacks simultaneously. Scenarios: HTTP + gRPC sharing runtime, WebSocket upgrade from HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2 + pool + TLS, concurrent TCP + UDP, timer-driven timeouts during I/O, graceful shutdown across all protocols. Log interleaved protocol activity with correlation IDs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:55.498314269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:36:16.039969169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:36:16.039870135Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1dgr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1phl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32ot","depends_on_id":"bd-2i3y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-32ot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cross-cutting integration tests are the final verification that protocol stacks don't interfere. Blocked on transport unit tests (bd-1qxg) and E2E logging (bd-1mgn) because multi-protocol tests need both foundations.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:12Z"},{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-32ot","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:52Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-330st","title":"Mechanize Small-Step Semantics","description":"Implement the small-step operational semantics in a proof assistant (Lean preferred, Coq acceptable).
Scope: regions, tasks, obligations, budgets, cancellation phases, and scheduler lanes.
Acceptance: executable semantics + machine-checked lemmas for well-formedness and progress of the operational rules.
Notes: keep the model minimal but faithful; use abstracted time and budgets as algebraic structures (min-plus) rather than concrete clocks.","notes":"Start plan (2026-02-03):\n- Existing Lean skeleton in formal/lean/Asupersync.lean defines core domains plus enqueue/schedule/spawn/complete rules.\n- Source of truth: asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md sections on obligations, cancellation, ledger, quiescence.\n\nPlanned increments:\n1) Extend state model with helper predicates (Held, Ledger, Quiescent).\n2) Add obligation transitions to Step: reserve, commit, abort, leak with holder-only guards.\n3) Add cancellation transitions: cancel-request, cancel-propagate, cancel-drain, finalize, and region-close preconditions.\n4) Add scheduler invariants (lane selection, runnable rules).\n5) Prove basic safety lemmas: obligation resolve-or-leak, region close implies ledger empty, cancellation monotonicity.\n6) Add regression lemmas for minimal well-formedness.\n\nFirst concrete coding step: implement obligation rules + helper functions, then add small lemmas that commit/abort eliminate reserved obligations.\n\nProgress (2026-02-03):\n- Added helper predicates removeObligationId/holdsObligation.\n- Added Step rules for reserve/commit/abort/leak with ledger updates.\n- Added cancellation skeleton: strengthenReason/strengthenOpt, cancelRequest, cancelMasked, cancelAcknowledge, cancelFinalize, cancelComplete.\n- Updated TaskState to carry cancel reason through cancelling/finalizing; Region now includes cancel field.\n- Added Close rule for quiescent regions.\n- Added cancel propagation and cancel-child rules (parent cancel pushes ParentCancelled to subregions and marks children CancelRequested).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyDesert","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:03.090134871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:49:42.728770384Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-330st","depends_on_id":"bd-2497b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:03.101197189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Scope: regions, tasks, obligations, budgets, cancellation phases, and scheduler lanes.
Acceptance: executable semantics + machine-checked lemmas for well-formedness and progress of the operational rules.
Notes: keep the model minimal but faithful; use abstracted time and budgets as algebraic structures (min-plus) rather than concrete clocks.","notes":"Start plan (2026-02-03):\n- Existing Lean skeleton in formal/lean/Asupersync.lean defines core domains plus enqueue/schedule/spawn/complete rules.\n- Source of truth: asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md sections on obligations, cancellation, ledger, quiescence.\n\nPlanned increments:\n1) Extend state model with helper predicates (Held, Ledger, Quiescent).\n2) Add obligation transitions to Step: reserve, commit, abort, leak with holder-only guards.\n3) Add cancellation transitions: cancel-request, cancel-propagate, cancel-drain, finalize, and region-close preconditions.\n4) Add scheduler invariants (lane selection, runnable rules).\n5) Prove basic safety lemmas: obligation resolve-or-leak, region close implies ledger empty, cancellation monotonicity.\n6) Add regression lemmas for minimal well-formedness.\n\nFirst concrete coding step: implement obligation rules + helper functions, then add small lemmas that commit/abort eliminate reserved obligations.\n\nProgress (2026-02-03):\n- Added helper predicates removeObligationId/holdsObligation.\n- Added Step rules for reserve/commit/abort/leak with ledger updates.\n- Added cancellation skeleton: strengthenReason/strengthenOpt, cancelRequest, cancelMasked, cancelAcknowledge, cancelFinalize, cancelComplete.\n- Updated TaskState to carry cancel reason through cancelling/finalizing; Region now includes cancel field.\n- Added Close rule for quiescent regions.\n- Added cancel propagation and cancel-child rules (parent cancel pushes ParentCancelled to subregions and marks children CancelRequested).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyDesert","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:03.090134871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:35:46.070902679Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-330st","depends_on_id":"bd-2497b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:03.101197189Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33k5","title":"[ALIEN][PERF] Opportunity matrix tracking + perf regression guardrails","description":"## Purpose

Make performance work disciplined:
- only implement Score >= 2.0 (Impact*Confidence/Effort)
- one lever per commit
- behavior unchanged (golden traces)

## Deliverables

- A lightweight template (doc or issue comment pattern) for recording:
  - baseline metrics
  - profile hotspot
  - opportunity score
  - isomorphism proof (ordering/tie-break/RNG)

## Acceptance criteria

- Contributors can follow a single checklist and produce comparable perf work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedWaterfall","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:09.621584971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:33:00.723123635Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:33:00.723048476Z","close_reason":"Opportunity matrix scoring framework added to docs/benchmarking.md: Score >= 2.0 threshold, one lever per commit policy, full opportunity template with baseline/profile/isomorphism proof sections","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33k5","depends_on_id":"bd-1i4s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-33k5","depends_on_id":"bd-2972","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3490","title":"[ALIEN][PERF] Golden traces + isomorphism proof harness","description":"## Purpose

Extreme optimization requires *behavior proofs*, not intuition.

We need stable golden artifacts that let us assert: "after optimization, behavior is isomorphic" (same observable outcomes, same determinism guarantees, same tie-breaking).

## Deliverables

- A golden-trace fixture format (minimal, stable across versions):
  - seed/config
  - trace fingerprint
  - selected canonicalized trace (or prefix) for sanity
  - invariant-oracle results
- A checksum/verification helper used by tests/benches (deterministic, no ambient state).

## Acceptance criteria

- Can add a golden fixture for a test program and verify it deterministically.
- Failing verification produces actionable diff output (which part changed: fingerprint, event count, etc.).
- No new dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:10:08.447683662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:51:58.011219567Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:51:58.011143115Z","close_reason":"Golden trace fixture format (GoldenTraceFixture) implemented in src/trace/format.rs with: seed/config snapshot, canonical fingerprint, Foata-layer prefix, oracle violation summary. Verification helper (GoldenTraceDiff) produces actionable field-level diffs. Tested in tests/golden_outputs.rs with deterministic lab workloads. No new dependencies.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3490","depends_on_id":"bd-1i4s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:13:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
